---
title: "Microbial Abundance and Prevalence"
author: "Dipankor Dhrubo"
date: "2025-09-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(survminer)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(DESeq2)
library(igraph)
library(purrr)
library(rlang)
library(mt.surv)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(tibble)
```

```{r}

## loading the Candida count file

Abundance<- 
  read_csv("/fs/ess/PAS1695/projects/exotho/data/THO_decontaminated-counts_taxonomy_2025-05-05.csv.gz") %>%
  select(-1)


## Filtering out irrelevant genus
## calculation and generating the plot

Top_Abundant_microbe<- Abundance %>%
  mutate(genus = str_remove(genus, "^[gG]__")) %>%                  # normalize
  filter(                                                           # drop Homo + any unclassified
    !str_detect(genus, regex("^Homo$", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    !str_detect(genus, regex("unclassified", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  group_by(genus) %>%
  summarise(sum.counts = sum(counts), .groups = "drop") %>%
  slice_max(sum.counts, n = 20, with_ties = FALSE)


Top_20_microbes <- Top_Abundant_microbe %>% pull(genus)

abundance_plot <- Top_Abundant_microbe %>%              # Considering the top 20 abundant Microbes 
  mutate(genus = fct_reorder(genus, sum.counts)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = genus, y = sum.counts, fill = genus)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_number(scale_cut = scales::cut_si(" ")), 
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .05))
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +     
  labs(
    title = "Microbial Abundance in NSCLC ORIEN",
    x = "Genus",
    y = "Total Counts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )


ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLC_Abundance_plot.png",abundance_plot, width = 12, height = 10)

#### Microbial Prevalence 


genus_presence <- Abundance %>%
  group_by(sample, genus) %>%
  summarise(total_counts = sum(counts, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(present = total_counts > 70)

# Compute prevalence across samples (fraction 0–1 and percent)
prevalence_tbl <- genus_presence %>%
  group_by(genus) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(genus = str_remove(genus, "^[gG]__")) %>%
  filter(
    !str_detect(genus, regex("^Homo$", ignore_case = TRUE)), 
    !str_detect(genus, regex("unclassified", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(prevalence))

# Keep only top 20 by prevalence
prevalence_top20 <- prevalence_tbl %>%
  slice_max(prevalence, n = 20, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(genus = fct_reorder(genus, prevalence))

## plotting the prevalance plot
cc_prev <- ggplot(prevalence_top20,
                  aes(x = genus, y = prevalence, fill = genus)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", end = 0.95) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Genus",
    y = "Prevalence (% of samples)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLC_Prevalence_plot.png",cc_prev, width = 12, height = 10)

```



```{r}

#### Mtsurv plots for most abundant and prevalent microbes

## Take the common between Abundant and prevalent microbes
Most_Abundand_Prev_microbe<- intersect(Top_Abundant_microbe$genus, prevalence_top20$genus)


### Creating a data from raw abundance file for mtsurv analysis

mtsurv_data<- Abundance %>%
  mutate(genus = str_remove(genus, "^[gG]__")) %>%                  # normalize
  filter(                                                           # drop Homo + any unclassified
    !str_detect(genus, regex("^Homo$", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    !str_detect(genus, regex("unclassified", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  group_by(sample,genus) %>%
  summarise(sum.counts = sum(counts), .groups = "drop") ## making sure there are no duplicates


### Loading new survival data 

processessed_data<- read_rds("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/Lung_resampled_data.rds") %>% select(sample,status,diagsurvtime)

mtsurv_data_2<- mtsurv_data %>% left_join(processessed_data, by="sample") %>% drop_na()
colnames(mtsurv_data_2)[4:5]<- c("vitalstatus","days")

genus_levels <- unique(mtsurv_data_2$genus)

# map plain names to the g__-prefixed names used in data
map_name <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x)) return(NA_character_)
  if (x %in% genus_levels) return(x)
  gx <- paste0("g__", x)
  if (gx %in% genus_levels) return(gx)
  NA_character_
}

## Just added "g__" before the names in Most_Abundand_Prev_microbe
species_mapped <- Most_Abundant_Prev_microbe %>%
  as.character() %>% tidyr::replace_na("") %>%
  lapply(map_name) %>% unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>%
  stats::na.omit() %>% unique()

# same simple structure you had, with tiny robustness tweaks

# This function ensures to avoid empty or one-level groups

safe_run <- function(genus_name) {
  # counts per sample (join zeros for absent)
  ab <- mtsurv_data_2 %>%
    filter(genus == genus_name) %>%
    group_by(sample) %>%
    summarise(counts = sum(sum.counts, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  input.tax <- clin_df %>%
    select(sample) %>%
    left_join(ab, by = "sample") %>%
    mutate(counts = replace_na(counts, 0)) %>%
    arrange(sample)
  
  # guards: need variation & two event states overall
  if (sd(input.tax$counts, na.rm = TRUE) == 0L || length(unique(clin_df$vitalstatus)) < 2L)
    return(NULL)
  
  # candidate cuts and keep only those strictly between min/max
  qs   <- seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
  cuts <- quantile(input.tax$counts, qs, na.rm = TRUE, type = 7)
  ok   <- cuts > min(input.tax$counts, na.rm = TRUE) & cuts < max(input.tax$counts, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # further require both arms exist and (optionally) both have events
  if (any(ok)) {
    cuts_ok <- cuts[ok]
    ok2 <- vapply(cuts_ok, function(ct) {
      g  <- input.tax$counts >= ct
      n0 <- sum(!g); n1 <- sum(g)
      if (n0 < 2 || n1 < 2) return(FALSE)
      e0 <- sum(clin_df$vitalstatus[!g] > 0); e1 <- sum(clin_df$vitalstatus[g] > 0)
      e0 > 0 && e1 > 0
    }, logical(1))
    qs <- qs[ok][ok2]
  } else {
    qs <- numeric(0)
  }
  if (!length(qs)) return(NULL)
  
  mt.surv::survivalByQuantile(
    input.var   = "counts",
    input.tax   = input.tax,
    surv.dat    = clin_df,
    percentiles = qs
  )
}


threshold.mat.by.microbe <- setNames(
  lapply(species_mapped, function(m) tryCatch(safe_run(m), error = function(e) {message(m, ": ", e$message); NULL})),
  nm = species_mapped
)



# full survival frame (one row per sample)
clin_df <- mtsurv_data_2 %>%
  distinct(sample, vitalstatus, days) %>%
  mutate(vitalstatus = as.integer(as.character(vitalstatus))) %>%
  arrange(sample)



all_microbe_df <- map2_dfr(
  threshold.mat.by.microbe,
  names(threshold.mat.by.microbe),
  ~ mutate(.x, microbe = .y)
)

## Mtsurv plotting 

ggplot(all_microbe_df, aes(x = percentile, y = -log10(pval), color = hazard.direction)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray30") +
  facet_wrap(~ microbe, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Microbial MTsurv_plots:Most Prevalant and Abundant",
    x = "Percentile",
    y = expression(-log[10](p))
  ) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
        legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLC_Microbial MTsurv_plots.png", width = 12, height = 10)


####### Area under the curve
calculateArea <- function(taxlev, threshold.mat, alpha = 0.05){
  df <- threshold.mat[[taxlev]] %>%
    dplyr::filter(pval < alpha)
  
  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      survival.effect = as.character("none"),
      sig.area = 0,
      species = taxlev
    ))
  }
  
  output <- threshold.mat[[taxlev]]%>%
    dplyr::filter(pval < alpha) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sig.dif = log(alpha) - log(pval),
                  survival.effect = ifelse(hazard.direction == "<1", "positive", "negative")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(survival.effect) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(sig.area = sum(sig.dif))%>%
    dplyr::mutate(species = taxlev)
  
  return(output)
}


## calculating area under the curve for each microbe
area_summary_df <- map_dfr(
  names(threshold.mat.by.microbe),
  function(cancer) {
    tryCatch({
      calculateArea(cancer, threshold.mat.by.microbe)
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("Error in", cancer, ":", e$message))
      return(NULL)
    })
  }
)


## Calculating sample size
sample_sizes_df <- dplyr::count(mtsurv_data_2, genus, name = "sample_size") %>%
  filter(genus %in% species_mapped)


area_summary_df <- area_summary_df %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes_df, by = c("species" = "genus"))



# Set factor levels for species ordering by total area
area_summary_df <- area_summary_df %>%
  mutate(species = factor(species, levels = unique(species[order(sig.area)])))


# Prepare data for plotting
area_summary_df <- area_summary_df %>%
  mutate(
    species = factor(species, levels = unique(species[order(sig.area)])),
    area_plot = case_when(
      survival.effect == "positive" ~ -sig.area,  # Plot left
      survival.effect == "negative" ~ sig.area,   # Plot right
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

## Including the prevalent data in the plot

prevalence_top20<- prevalence_top20 %>% filter(genus %in% area_summary_df$species)
names(prevalence_top20)[1]<- "species"

## Including the Abundant data in the plot
abundance_data<- abundance_plot$data

abundance_data <- abundance_data %>% filter(genus %in% area_summary_df$species)
names(abundance_data)[1]<- "species"

area_summary_df<- area_summary_df %>% left_join(prevalence_top20,by="species") %>%
  left_join(abundance_data,by="species")


# --- 0) ONE ordering for all panels (rank by net area; change if you prefer) ---
species_order <- area_summary_df %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(area_net = sum(ifelse(survival.effect == "negative", sig.area, -sig.area), na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(area_net)) %>%
  pull(species)

# --- 1) Per-species metadata for LEFT & MIDDLE (no duplicates) ---
meta_df <- area_summary_df %>%
  distinct(species, sample_size, prevalence, sum.counts) %>%
  mutate(
    prevalence  = replace_na(prevalence, 0),
    sum.counts  = replace_na(sum.counts, 0),
    species     = factor(species, levels = species_order)
  )

# --- 2) Right panel data (keep both effects) ---
right_df <- area_summary_df %>%
  mutate(
    species = factor(species, levels = species_order),
    survival.effect = factor(survival.effect, levels = c("negative","positive"))
  )

# === Left: PREVALENCE (to the left) ===
p_prev <- ggplot(meta_df) +
  geom_col(aes(x = -prevalence, y = species), fill = "#61A9D9", alpha = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) percent(abs(x))) +
  labs(x = "Prevalence", y = "Microbe") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 11,face = "bold.italic"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# === Middle: TOTAL COUNTS (to the left) ===
p_sum <- ggplot(meta_df) +
  geom_col(aes(x = -sum.counts, y = species), fill = "#797CBA", alpha = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) comma(abs(x))) +
  labs(x = "Abundance", y = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# === Right: AREA (your survival impact) ===
p_area <- ggplot(right_df) +
  geom_col(aes(x = area_plot, y = species, fill = survival.effect), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("positive" = "orange", "negative" = "#1F3E71")) +
  labs(x = "Microbial Survival Impact", y = NULL, fill = "Survival Effect") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11,face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# === Combine ===
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

library(forcats)

# after you build meta_df and right_df (or area_summary_df), do:
meta_df  <- meta_df  %>% mutate(species = fct_rev(species))
right_df <- right_df %>% mutate(species = fct_rev(species))

# then re-run p_prev, p_sum, p_area exactly as before

final_plot <- p_prev + p_sum + p_area + plot_layout(ncol = 3, widths = c(1, 1, 1.2))
final_plot <- (p_prev + p_sum + p_area +
                 plot_layout(ncol = 3, widths = c(1, 1, 1.2))) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Microbial Prevalence, Abundance, and Survival impact",
    caption = "α = 0.05",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = 9)
    )
  )

final_plot

ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLC_Microbial_rank.png",plot = final_plot, width = 12, height = 10)

```
